com.mentor.chs.api.report

Interface IXReportModule


  • public interface IXReportModule
    A report module clubs together collection of queries and result expressions. A report module typically corresponds to a table of a report, with result expressions as the column definitions. The queries define the element type that are to be reported. The result expressions defined in a report module evaluate on each of the objects returned by queries, in effect defining what goes in to each column of a table.

    • Method Summary

    • Method Detail

      • getQueries

        java.util.Collection<IXQuery> getQueries()
        Returns the queries for this module.
        Returns:
        collection of queries that define this report module.

      • addQuery

        void addQuery(IXQuery query,
              java.lang.String queryTag)
        add a query to report module with an optional tag to query. A report module can have duplicate queries for a particular entity type. To differentiate the results from duplicate queries, queries can be tagged in a module. These tags are optional and may be empty(""). On evaluation of a report these tags as used as hints for result expressions(see IXResultExpression:evaluate( IXObject entity, String executionContext) to evaluate differently on objects from queries for the same entity type. For example in component BOM report, one can query for IXCavityDetail twice in a module, and then while evaluating the result expression for displaying partnumber the query tag is passed as a hint to show SealPartNumber for the IXCavityDetail got from the first query and TerminalPartNumber for the IXCavityDetail resulting from the second query.
        Parameters:
        query - implementing IXQuery interface
        queryTag - tag the result set with a name.(can be empty or null)

      • getQueryTags

        java.util.Set<java.lang.String> getQueryTags()
        Returns:
        user specified types on query types.see addQuery for description on tags

      • getQueriesForTag

        java.util.Collection<IXQuery> getQueriesForTag(java.lang.String queryTag)
        Parameters:
        queryTag - -- user specifed tag
        Returns:
        all queries for which its return type tag matches queryTag provided. tagging query results is useful for conditional evaluation of results for query.

      • getReportTemplates

        java.util.Collection<IXReportTemplate> getReportTemplates()
        Returns the ordered list of report templates defined for this report. Report templates are stored in an ordered manner and the same order is reflected in the report generated.
        Returns:
        resultset

      • getTitle

        java.lang.String getTitle()
        returns the title.
        Returns:
        title

      • addCustomAttribute

        void addCustomAttribute(java.lang.String name,
                        java.lang.String value)
        add a custom attribute name, value for this report template
        Parameters:
        name - -- attribute name
        value - -- attribute value

      • getCustomAttributeValue

        java.lang.String getCustomAttributeValue(java.lang.String attributeName)
        get custom attribute value for the given attribute name. Custom attribute
        Parameters:
        attributeName - -- custom atttibute name of which value is sought.
        Returns:
        custom attribute value corresponding to attributeName.

      • getCustomAttributes

        java.util.Set<java.lang.String> getCustomAttributes()
        Access user defined attribute names.
        Returns:
        set of user defined attributes on this report module.

      • addCustomTag

        void addCustomTag(java.lang.String tag,
                  java.util.Map<java.lang.String,java.lang.String> attributes)
        add a user defined tag. This is useful typically as a hint to xml formatter after xml report is generated. For example a custom tag 'groupBy' with attribute title='XXX' might indicate which column to group by while formatting. Note: tag names need not be distinct.
        Parameters:
        tag - -- name of the custom tag
        attributes - -- as name value pairs

      • getCustomTags

        java.util.Set<java.lang.String> getCustomTags()
        get all user defined tags in the report module definition. For use of tags IXReportModule:addCustomTag
        Returns:
        set of names of tags.

      • getAttributesForCustomTag

        java.util.List<java.util.Map<java.lang.String,java.lang.String>> getAttributesForCustomTag(java.lang.String tagName)
        list of name value pairs associated with a tag name. Since tags may be duplicated, there may be more than one entry in list returned.
        Parameters:
        tagName - --
        Returns:
        list of attribute-name attribute-value pairs.

      • addReportTemplate

        void addReportTemplate(IXReportTemplate reportTemplate)
        add a report template (column definition)
        Parameters:
        reportTemplate - -- report template to be added.

      • addReportModule

        void addReportModule(IXReportModule reportModule)
        modules could be nested by adding one within the other, use addReportModule to add a report module ( sub-module) within this module. In the evaluation of this report module, each object returned by queries associated with this module goes in as scope for queries defined in sub-module. So for example if this report module is a wire list that returns wires and a sub-module queries on connectors, wires become the scope for the sub-module query that returns connectors and so connectors related to wires are returned in evaluating the sub -module query. IXReportModule:getReportModules
        Parameters:
        reportModule - -- nested report module to be added.

      • getReportModules

        java.util.Collection<? extends IXReportModule> getReportModules()
        all sub-modules (nested modules )in this report module. IXReportModule:addReportModule
        Returns:
        a collection of nested report modules.

      • getGroupingExpressions

        java.util.List<IXResultExpression> getGroupingExpressions()
        get expressions to group query results by.
        Returns:
        an ordered list of result expressions with which to group query results

      • addGroupingExpression

        void addGroupingExpression(IXResultExpression resultExpression)
        add a grouping expression with which to group entities resulting from queries.
        Parameters:
        resultExpression - -- expression with which to group by.

      • getEvaluationScopeIndex

        int getEvaluationScopeIndex()
